specific.evidence: All requested functionality implemented: login, logout, token refresh endpoints. Auth middleware created and applied to protected routes.

measurable.evidence: All deliverables created. Tests passing with 92% coverage. Login/logout/refresh working end-to-end.
issues: Coverage just above threshold, could be improved

achievable.evidence: Solution uses existing Express framework and JWT library. Code follows project conventions and is maintainable.

relevant.evidence: Directly addresses authentication requirement. Follows architectural patterns. No conflicts with existing code.

time_bound.evidence: Completed in 1.5 hours (under 2-hour limit). All tests passing.
issues: Slightly faster than estimated, could have added more edge case tests

deliverables_completed.name: auth-middleware.ts
deliverables_completed.evidence: JWT verification middleware handles token validation, expiration, and user context extraction

deliverables_completed.name: auth-routes.ts
deliverables_completed.evidence: Login, logout, and refresh endpoints implemented with proper error handling

deliverables_completed.name: auth.test.ts
deliverables_completed.evidence: 15 tests covering all endpoints, middleware, and edge cases

acceptance_criteria_met.criterion: POST /api/auth/login returns JWT token on valid credentials
acceptance_criteria_met.evidence: Test: auth.test.ts:12 - returns 200 with access_token and refresh_token
Test: auth.test.ts:28 - refresh token blacklisted after logout
Test: auth.test.ts:45 - returns new access_token with valid refresh_token

acceptance_criteria_met.criterion: Protected routes return 401 without valid token
acceptance_criteria_met.evidence: Test: auth.test.ts:62 - returns 401 when Authorization header missing

issues_found.description: Test coverage is 92.3%, just above the 90% threshold
issues_found.resolution: Could add more edge case tests for token expiration scenarios
